description.value:
Rovari was said to be the first soul to willingly leave the sanctuary of the gods and walk among mortals not to rule them, but to learn from them. She saw each road as a thread in the great tapestry of Elara, and every experience — joy or sorrow — as sacred.

Some say she was the one who taught the first mortals how to unify tribes, how to ask questions, and how to forgive themselves when they failed.

During the Sundering, Rovari grieved—not out of hatred or fear—but because she knew the world would become impossible to explore for her. Still, she left a blessing in every crossroad, hoping her example would live on in those brave enough to walk forward without certainty.
